creator: karels@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Alex Dupre from comment #5)
I tested with the new port.  The chip is detected.  I gave it an address, then connected the cable, and got this panic:

# ifconfig re0 10.0.2.4/24
# re0: link state changed to DOWN
re0: link state changed to UP
re0: called ether_input w/o net epoch! PLEASE file a bug report.KDB: stack backtrace:
db_trace_self_wrapper() at db_trace_self_wrapper+0x2b/frame 0xfffffe0119775ca0
ether_input() at ether_input+0x68/frame 0xfffffe0119775d00
re_rxeof() at re_rxeof+0x33d/frame 0xfffffe0119775d80
re_int_task_8125() at re_int_task_8125+0xb5/frame 0xfffffe0119775dc0
taskqueue_run_locked() at taskqueue_run_locked+0xab/frame 0xfffffe0119775e40
taskqueue_run() at taskqueue_run+0x4d/frame 0xfffffe0119775e60
ithread_loop() at ithread_loop+0x266/frame 0xfffffe0119775ef0
fork_exit() at fork_exit+0x82/frame 0xfffffe0119775f30
fork_trampoline() at fork_trampoline+0xe/frame 0xfffffe0119775f30
--- trap 0xe546e546, rip = 0x12dc12dc12dc12dc, rsp = 0xd3b1d3b1d3b1d3b1, rbp = 0x3c133c133c133c13 ---
panic: Assertion in_epoch(net_epoch_preempt) failed at ../../../netinet/tcp_lro.c:1208
cpuid = 10
time = 1702992655
KDB: stack backtrace:
db_trace_self_wrapper() at db_trace_self_wrapper+0x2b/frame 0xfffffe0119775b20
vpanic() at vpanic+0x132/frame 0xfffffe0119775c50
panic() at panic+0x43/frame 0xfffffe0119775cb0
tcp_lro_flush_all() at tcp_lro_flush_all+0x221/frame 0xfffffe0119775d00
re_rxeof() at re_rxeof+0x411/frame 0xfffffe0119775d80
re_int_task_8125() at re_int_task_8125+0xb5/frame 0xfffffe0119775dc0
taskqueue_run_locked() at taskqueue_run_locked+0xab/frame 0xfffffe0119775e40
taskqueue_run() at taskqueue_run+0x4d/frame 0xfffffe0119775e60
ithread_loop() at ithread_loop+0x266/frame 0xfffffe0119775ef0
fork_exit() at fork_exit+0x82/frame 0xfffffe0119775f30
fork_trampoline() at fork_trampoline+0xe/frame 0xfffffe0119775f30
--- trap 0xe546e546, rip = 0x12dc12dc12dc12dc, rsp = 0xd3b1d3b1d3b1d3b1, rbp = 0x3c133c133c133c13 ---
KDB: enter: panic
[ thread pid 12 tid 100081 ]
Stopped at      kdb_enter+0x32: movq    $0,0xe30933(%rip)
db> 

Just for completeness, the pciconf -lv is:

re0@pci0:5:0:0: class=0x020000 rev=0x05 hdr=0x00 vendor=0x10ec device=0x8125 subvendor=0x1458 subdevice=0xe000
    vendor     = 'Realtek Semiconductor Co., Ltd.'
    device     = 'RTL8125 2.5GbE Controller'
    class      = network
    subclass   = ethernet
